definition: Private credit return smoothing (0.5-0.7 NAV autocorrelation) understates portfolio volatility by 15-25% across $5T+ in institutional assets that hold private credit alongside public markets, contaminating risk budgeting across pension funds and insurers.
evidence: NAV autocorrelation of 0.5-0.7 produces calculable vol understatement
Behavioral assumptions about risk budget reactions debated
open_questions: How do institutions actually respond when true volatility is revealed?
Does vol understatement lead to over-allocation or is it already priced in?
